hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity in the ECL2/TM5 interface, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) binding by avoiding electrostatic interactions with conserved acidic residues in those subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is insufficient to achieve robust SSTR2 selectivity. The focus must shift to explicitly targeting non-pharmacophore positions for mutagenesis to reduce off-target binding.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve SSTR2 selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 without compromising the native pharmacophore or stability.
